pots_identified.description: Yellow/tan pot, visible in EARLIEST, T-5, T-4. Appears to contain soil.
Dark pot, visible in EARLIEST, T-5, T-4. Appears to contain soil with white fragments.
Dark pot, visible in T-1, CURRENT. Contains a small plant and soil with white fragments. This pot is a new introduction to the scene.

compositional_truth_check.scale_anchor_status: The white rabbit (5cm) scale anchor, expected in P2 (registry) or P3 (prompt detail), is not visible in any of the provided images.

P2_mexican_mint.registry_status: Expected: Mexican Mint in P2 (Black Pot | White Rabbit anchor).
P2_mexican_mint.visual_status: In EARLIEST, T-5, T-4, P2 is a dark pot, but no plant identifiable as Mexican Mint is visible; only soil and white fragments. From T-3 onwards, the original P2 pot is absent.
P2_mexican_mint.reconciliation: Systemic Loss. The registered Mexican Mint was not visually confirmed, and the pot associated with it (P2) has been removed from the scene.

P3_unidentified_plant.visual_status: A new dark pot (P3) containing a small, broad-leafed plant is present in T-1 and CURRENT. This plant does not resemble typical Mexican Mint.
P3_unidentified_plant.reconciliation: New Introduction/Intervention. An uncatalogued plant specimen has been introduced into a new pot (P3).

plant_audit.status_earliest_to_t4: Contains dark, somewhat moist soil with faint dry organic matter on the surface. No active plant growth or identifiable specimen.
plant_audit.status_t3_to_current: Systemic Loss. The pot is no longer present in the scene.

plant_audit.status_earliest_to_t4: Contains dark, somewhat moist soil with several white fragments (likely eggshells) on the surface. No active plant growth or identifiable specimen matching Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.status_t3_to_current: Systemic Loss. The pot is no longer present in the scene.

plant_audit.status_t1_to_current: Contains a small, unidentified broad-leafed plant. The plant has approximately 3-4 visible, ovate, dark green leaves with a smooth texture. The stem appears upright. The soil is dark and moist, with white fragments (eggshells) scattered on the surface. No incidental growth observed. The plant appears healthy and turgid.

biome_observations.soil_texture: In EARLIEST, T-5, T-4, soil in P1 and P2 appears dark and somewhat moist. In T-1 and CURRENT, soil in P3 is dark and moist. No visible signs of cracking or desiccation in any observed soil.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: No visible fungal growth or mycelial networks observed in any image.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, secondary seedlings, or uncatalogued sprouts are observed in the soil of any pot throughout the sequence.
biome_observations.desk_debris: White fragments (likely eggshells) are consistently present on the soil surface of P2 (earlier images) and P3 (later images). Wires are visible near P1 in earlier images. In later images (T-1, CURRENT), a blue book, a white pen, and an electronic sensor board with connected wires are present on the desk surface.

temporal_deltas.changes_from_previous: Initial state. The scene shows two pots: P1 (yellow/tan, top-right) and P2 (dark, bottom-left). Both contain dark soil. P2's soil surface has white fragments (eggshells). No visible plants. A white cylindrical object and wires are near P1.

Minimal change. The overall scene brightness has slightly increased, allowing for a marginally clearer view of the soil in P1, where faint dry organic matter is discernible. The composition and contents of the pots remain otherwise identical.

Further improvement in lighting and image clarity. The soil texture in P1 is more distinct, possibly indicating slight surface drying or rearrangement. The white cylindrical object near P1 is also clearer. No significant changes to plant presence or pot contents.
Complete loss of visual data. The image is entirely black, indicating a camera malfunction, power loss, or complete environmental darkness. The previous setup (P1, P2) is no longer visible.
No change from T-3. The image remains entirely black, indicating the same conditions of data loss persist.

Dramatic scene transformation. The previous pots (P1, P2) are gone, representing a 'Systemic Loss'. A new setup is present, featuring a single dark pot (P3) containing a small, unidentified broad-leafed plant, which is a 'New Introduction'. New ancillary items, including a blue book, a white pen, and an electronic sensor board with wires, are visible on the desk. The soil in P3 contains white fragments.

No discernible change from T-1. The unidentified plant in P3, its pot, and the surrounding desk objects appear identical. There are no visible signs of growth, wilting, or discoloration within this short timeframe.

visual_health_inference.pots_p1_p2_earliest_to_t4: No plant life was visually present in these pots, so no health inference can be made regarding plants. The soil appeared adequately moist.
visual_health_inference.unidentified_plant_p3_t1_to_current: The plant appears to be in good health. Its leaves are dark green, turgid, and show no signs of wilting, chlorosis, necrosis, or pest damage. The soil appears moist, suggesting adequate hydration. The cool spectrum LED and diffuse north window light seem to be sufficient for its current state.

anomalies: Missing Registry Plant: The 'Mexican Mint' registered for P2 was not visually identifiable in any image where P2 was present, and P2 itself was subsequently removed from the scene.
Missing Scale Anchor: The 'White rabbit (5cm)' scale anchor was not observed in any image, despite being listed in the world model.
Data Loss: Images T-3 and T-2 are completely black, representing a significant and unexplained period of visual data loss.

Scene Transformation: A complete and abrupt change in the experimental setup occurred between T-4 and T-1, involving the removal of the original pots and the introduction of a new pot, plant, and surrounding objects. This constitutes a major intervention.
Unidentified Plant: The plant introduced in P3 (T-1, CURRENT) is not listed in the 'EXPECTED BIOME REGISTRY' and its species remains unidentified.
vision_report.narrative_description: The initial phase of observation (EARLIEST to T-4) revealed a setup with two pots, P1 and P2. P1 contained bare soil, while P2, designated for Mexican Mint in the registry, also appeared to contain only soil with eggshell fragments, with no identifiable plant. A critical data gap followed (T-3, T-2), where images were entirely black. Subsequently, a dramatic and complete transformation of the scene occurred. The original pots (P1 and P2) were removed, signifying a 'Systemic Loss' of the registered biome. A new setup was introduced (T-1, CURRENT), featuring a single dark pot (P3) containing a small, unidentified broad-leafed plant. This new plant, a 'New Introduction', appears healthy and turgid, with dark green leaves and moist soil. Surrounding the pot are new desk items including a book, a pen, and electronic components. The white rabbit scale anchor was not observed at any point in the sequence. While the currently present plant exhibits good health, the continuity and integrity of the original biome registry have been severely compromised by the scene changes and data loss.
